rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component type or implementation scope as required by the case
Preserve existing AADL architecture by only inserting annex blocks, not modifying other elements
Use visible identifiers from the case when available, otherwise use case-provided names
Do not insert annex blocks that reference subcomponent ports in component type scopes
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific scope that contains all referenced identifiers
Preserve existing component type and implementation boundaries
Do not modify existing connections or subcomponents
Use case-provided names for identifiers when no visible whitelist is available
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax
Component type annexes contain assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees that reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
Do not insert annex blocks that would create type mismatches
